title: N-p-Tolyl Oxamic Acid as Analytical Reagent for Uranyl [UO2(II)]
author: A. K. SINGH
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Chemical Laboratories, Agra College, Agra 

  Manuscript received 24 December 1980, revised 11 May 1981, accepted 15 July 1981  

 N- p -tolyl oxamic acid (NPTOXA) has been successfully used as an analytical reagent for gravimetric estimation of UO 2 (II) and its separation, using a number or masking agents from some metal ions resulting from the radioactive disintegration and consequently occurring in uranium ores.
